mcqs.question: Which type of hypervisor runs directly on the host's hardware?
mcqs.explanation: Type 1 hypervisors, also known as bare-metal hypervisors, run directly on the host's hardware without requiring a host operating system.

mcqs.question: Which technology uses CPU extensions (Intel VT-x, AMD-V) to improve virtualization performance?
mcqs.explanation: Hardware-assisted virtualization uses CPU extensions like Intel VT-x and AMD-V to improve performance by reducing the overhead of the hypervisor.

mcqs.question: Which memory virtualization technique uses hardware support to map guest physical memory to host physical memory?
mcqs.explanation: Extended Page Tables (EPT) is a hardware-assisted memory virtualization technique that provides efficient mapping between guest physical memory and host physical memory.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between full virtualization and paravirtualization?

options.text: Full virtualization requires modified guest OS, while paravirtualization doesn't
Paravirtualization requires modified guest OS, while full virtualization doesn't
Full virtualization is faster than paravirtualization
Paravirtualization uses hardware extensions while full virtualization doesn't

mcqs.explanation: Paravirtualization requires the guest operating system to be modified to be aware of the virtualization layer, while full virtualization can run unmodified guest operating systems.

mcqs.question: Which virtualization feature allows running VMs to be moved from one physical host to another without downtime?
mcqs.explanation: Live migration (e.g., vMotion in VMware, Live Migration in Hyper-V) allows running virtual machines to be moved between physical hosts without service interruption.
